mnemonics.title: OOP Pillars
mnemonics.content: Remember OOP principles with: 'CIA' - C for Encapsulation, I for Inheritance, A for Abstraction (add P for Polymorphism = CIPA)

mnemonics.title: Class vs Object
mnemonics.content: Class = Blueprint (plan), Object = House (actual). Blueprint exists once, many houses can be built from it.

mnemonics.title: Constructor Purpose
mnemonics.content: Constructors 'Construct' objects - they build/initialize new instances. Remember: Constructor = Create + Initialize

acronyms.usage: Programming paradigm based on objects containing data and code
Methodology for software design using OOP principles
Simple Java objects not bound to special frameworks

visualTips.visualization: Draw a class as a rectangle with two sections: top for attributes (nouns), bottom for methods (verbs). Below it, show multiple object instances as smaller rectangles.
Imagine a medicine capsule - it contains all ingredients (data+methods) inside, with only the outer surface (interface) accessible.
Draw an inverted tree: superclass at top, connected by lines to subclasses below. Arrow points from subclass to superclass.
Draw different shapes (circle, square, triangle) all responding to same 'draw()' message differently - like buttons on a remote controlling different devices.

analogies.analogy: Class is like a recipe card, Object is like the actual cake made using that recipe. One recipe can make many cakes.
Like a TV remote - you press buttons (public interface) without knowing the internal electronics (hidden implementation).
Like a child inheriting eye color from parents - characteristics (attributes/methods) passed down from parent class to child class.
Like driving a car - you use steering, pedals, gears (abstracted interface) without understanding the internal engine mechanics.
Like a remote control button 'Power' - it turns on TV, AC, or fan depending on which device it's controlling.
